segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a dramatic aerial shot of a cityscape under a stormy sky, overlaid with a glowing green map of the UK and the text 'TODAY ON BETA SQUAD'. A deep male voiceover announces, "Today on Beta Squad, they are racing to see who can get the furthest away from London." The scene transitions to a bright, stylized graphic of the UK map with 'LONDON' marked, as the voiceover continues, explaining the teams are "split into two teams." Team 1, featuring Chunkz, a man in a patterned sweater and black beanie, and AJ, a man in a dark hoodie and cap, is introduced visually in a lively urban setting with bold text overlays. During their introduction, Chunkz emits an "Ow!" sound, followed by AJ exclaiming, "Oh, what's going on?" The voiceover then introduces "Team 2, which has Nico, Sharky, and Kenny," while visually Sharky, a man in a black jacket and cap, and Shroom, a man in a dark jacket and beanie, are seen riding bicycles and interacting energetically on a city street. A lively "Gather 'round, gather 'round!" is heard from one of the team members. The teams are then shown receiving instructions from a host, a man in a dark jacket with a fur-lined hood, as the voiceover states they will be given "a range of different challenges for them to do and must complete before the time runs out." A countdown timer appears against a sunset backdrop, showing '09:59:57', with the text 'THEY HAVE EXACTLY 10 HOURS', as the voiceover confirms they have "exactly 10 hours to see how far they can get." The challenges are then revealed in a series of blue-tinted, glitch-effect title cards: Challenge 1 requires stroking an animal with video proof; Challenge 2 involves convincing a member of the public that something is a famous landmark and taking a picture with them; and Challenge 3 mandates using five different modes of transport, after which they can use any vehicle, all while adhering to a budget of "69 pounds per person." The segment ends with a transition to Chunkz, signaling the start of the action, as the voiceover begins to say, "Now, let's go to Chunkz to..."

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens dynamically at Piccadilly Circus in London, England, with an energetic background track and the sounds of a bustling city. The Host, dressed in a black jacket with a fur-lined hood and a black beanie, greets the teams with a high-five, loudly initiating the challenge and declaring, "the challenge starts now!" He instructs them to go north by train, aiming for Manchester or Liverpool. AJ, wearing a dark hoodie and a black cap with a Jumpman logo, checks his phone, initially stating a train ticket costs "£71," though the visual context indicates the actual total cost is £213, which exceeds their £207 budget. Chunkz, in a colorful patterned sweater and black beanie, joins the discussion. The team quickly decides to start with a bike to Euston and then take a train, but first, they need to earn money. The Host confirms they have "an hour and 20" minutes until the train departs. As AJ and Chunkz walk through the busy London streets, passing traffic lights, pedestrians, and iconic architecture like a red phone booth, AJ confidently explains their plan to reach Euston Station and catch a train to Manchester. Chunkz, impressed, questions how AJ devised the plan so quickly, to which AJ confidently replies, "Because I'm ready, I'm on point today." The urgent city sounds and background music underscore their focused mission to make money before their departure, with the Host reminding them, "Time's already going."

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a fast-paced, blurred street view in London, transitioning to Chunkz, in a colorful patterned sweater and black beanie, exclaiming "Look how fast I am!" while riding an electric bike. He then reacts with a surprised "What the f-" after a near crash. AJ, in a dark hoodie and black Jumpman cap, rides alongside him, checking his phone. AJ states, "Okay, so we're on our first mode of transport, we're on bikes," and asks Chunkz, "When's the last time you were on a bike?" Chunkz replies, "Bro, years, it's been years," and adds, "I almost saw you crash when you went there." AJ acknowledges, "Yeah, I know, I know," and then comments, "You can't beep at him." On-screen text confirms their mode of transport as 'BIKE 1/5' and highlights their goal: 'WE'RE GOING TO EUSTON STATION'. AJ continues, detailing their plan: "We're gonna take the bike straight to Euston and we have our little phones in there now. I think 13 minutes, maybe 25 'cause it's me." He then urges someone, "Jump in, we're about to go, you're making us late, come on!" before declaring, "We're at the station!" The scene shifts to an aerial map displaying a green route from Piccadilly Circus to Euston Station, with the team's faces superimposed on the path, as a narrator's voiceover states, "As Team 1 begins their journey, the other team began their money laundering schemes." The narrative then cuts to a street scene in Piccadilly Circus where the Host, in a black jacket with a fur-lined hood and black beanie, holding a phone, confronts a group of three men dressed in black winter wear, including one in a bucket hat, and AJ. The Host playfully asks, "You guys have your passports?" and then offers, "I can teach you some spells for some money." One of the men from the group responds, "Um, so we're wizards, we go to the School of Harry Potter." The Host questions, "Are you sure?" to which the man replies, "Yeah, so we can teach spells for two pounds." The Host negotiates, "One pound is fine," and after receiving it, says, "Thank you, thank you." The Host then turns to a woman in a Gryffindor scarf and cloak, holding a wand, and playfully says, "Okay, I'll teach you a spell," prompting her to perform a magic trick with a smile, whispering the spell "Shishmania... Shishmania... It makes them disappear." On-screen text humorously labels the interaction as 'THEIR MONEY LAUNDERING SCHEME' and 'EXTORTING AN INNOCENT FAMILY', while the team's budget is displayed as £208. A narrator's voiceover sarcastically comments, "It seems that this team's moral compass is at an all-time low, as they extort this innocent family for one pound." The atmosphere is lighthearted and comedic, blending urban exploration with playful street interactions.

The Host, a man in a black jacket with a fur-lined hood and black beanie, playfully interacts with a Woman in Gryffindor Scarf, who wears a black coat and maroon scarf while holding a wand in front of the Hard Rock Cafe. A male narrator then provides context, stating, 'And then they happily break the law as they begin to put on a show without a legal permit.' It's rude to just walk past while people are performing,' and asks, 'Do you want to watch the boxing dancing show?' with a laugh. He then hypes up the performance, 'Let's go for that knockout blow! Three, two, one! Woo!' followed by 'And that's crazy dancing at the end!' The group concludes with a high-energy dance sequence and enthusiastic clapping, with the Host shouting, 'Yeah! Amidst the lively atmosphere, a male voice asks, 'So where's the money now?' followed by laughter. A Young Woman in a Red Hoodie approaches, takes a selfie, and clearly asks, 'Do you have a hat for the show?' to which the Host responds with a thumbs-up, before the group continues their performance near a 'HOME OF LA CLIQUE' sign.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with AJ, in a dark hoodie and black Jumpman cap, near Euston Station, where on-screen text displays the team’s budget (£143) and train ticket cost (£142). He interacts with a man in a blue jacket, asking "You got five pounds?" and gratefully accepting "Five pounds" from the man, exclaiming "Oh, thank you! Thank you very much!" AJ then states, "All right, cool, let's book these tickets and go to Manchester." The scene shifts to a bustling city plaza where Sharky, Shroom, and the Host, all in black winter jackets and beanies, are seen walking and talking. The Host asks a young woman in a red hoodie, "You guys from Liverpool?", to which she replies "Yeah," and expresses surprise with "Oh, really?" as the Host explains, "We're trying to get there today... we don't currently have the funds. If we can do a Scouse accent that impresses you, you give us a tiny bit of cash." They then encounter a woman in a black coat and maroon Gryffindor scarf; the Host asks her, "You got a tenner?", and she responds "Thank you." She performs a magic trick with a wand, astonishing the Host who shouts, "Oh my gosh! No way! No!" Sharky adds an "Oh!" of surprise, and the Host continues in disbelief, "What? I can't believe it! Why is he laughing? We can't believe it! Oh my gosh! We got a tenner!" He thanks her, "Oh, you've saved our day! We'll see you there! Thank you!" The group continues walking through the city, passing Christmas market signage and a Hard Rock Cafe. The Host calculates their updated budget, "So we got 69 pounds each, which means we got 207. Add the 11 pounds we just made, we got 218 pounds." They express relief and joy, pointing toward Euston Station, and the Host suggests, "Should we go towards the bikes now? Let's get some bikes! Take it to Euston." The scene ends with them walking past a row of docked electric bikes, with a red double-decker bus visible in the background, as a narrator states, "Upon finding the bikes, the boys rushed to hire them out, and the chaos ensues."

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens on a sunlit London street, where AJ, in a dark hoodie and black Jumpman cap, and the Host, later identified as Nico, in a black puffer jacket with a fur-lined hood, stand near a row of blue electric bikes. Nico confidently states, "Euston by bike is 13 minutes. We can shave off three minutes, easy. We can get there in 10 minutes, easy," before urging, "Let's go, let's go, let's go, let's go!" as he checks his phone and mounts a 'Lime' electric bike. AJ follows closely, and the camera tracks them weaving through city streets, past cars and pedestrians. The ride quickly becomes chaotic, with Nico exclaiming, "Oh my gosh, oh my gosh, careful, careful, careful. Oh, almost died immediately," after a near collision with a van. His panic escalates amidst an upbeat, tense background music, shouting, "We almost died this quick. Careful! I am a strong bike rider. I'm telling you now. Oh, guys! This is a mess! Oh my God, I'm about to fall in! I'm not confident! This is a bad idea! Ooooooh! Where's Sharky? I'm not enjoying this! I'm behind! This is the worst thing I've ever done! Oh, I almost jumped into a van! Feel like I'm gonna die. Feel like I'm gonna die." After a brief, shaky moment, they stop at a crosswalk. segments.integrated_caption: The video segment opens with the group—Host, AJ, Sharky, and Shroom—standing outside a modern building labeled 'The Podium,' visibly excited and celebrating their successful task. The Host, in his black puffer jacket with a fur-lined hood and black beanie, exclaims 'OH MY GOD WAIT' as they realize they’ve made their train, with AJ adding a quick 'Brother.' Amidst celebratory shouts and the Host's exclamations of 'Oh my god,' 'Thank you, man,' and 'Oh, amazing,' the group rushes through a city plaza, passing a 'TAXIS' sign and a 'DISCOVER' banner. The Host raises his arms in triumph, shouting 'MANNY HERE WE COME,' and declares, 'Guys, we are making our train!' with further celebratory cheers and shouts of 'Let's go!' and 'Woo!' as they enter Euston Station. They gather around a blue self-service ticket machine, checking their budget displayed on-screen as £215. AJ, in his dark hoodie and Jumpman cap, explains, 'More on there than it is on here, so we're gonna have to switch. £230 here, £218.10 there,' as he holds up his phone to show the team’s updated budget of £218.10, revealing they have an extra £2.79. He clarifies, 'We're in a bit of a situation. There's £2.49 tax, so we've only got an extra £2. So we now have to make 49p.' Sharky, in his black jacket and cap, reacts with surprise, while Shroom, in his dark jacket and beanie, gives a thumbs-up and confirms 'Yeah' when asked if he has 50p. The group celebrates their financial gain, with the Host exclaiming 'That is... Oh, that's even better,' and 'Because of you, we might win this challenge.' The Host then shouts, 'There we go, you're going to Manchester Piccadilly!' and 'Yes!' as they rush toward the platform, passing the 'Euston Station' signage and a large departure board. AJ adds, 'That's our second mode of transport done.' The Host urges, 'We got, we got eight minutes to catch our train. All right, let's go, let's go. Boarding, it's boarding, platform six!' Someone, likely AJ, adds, 'We actually might miss this,' and 'Get my cardio in for the day,' as the group moves quickly through the bustling station, their excitement palpable.

segments.integrated_caption: Inside a brightly lit, moving train, the lively atmosphere is filled with chatter and the rhythmic sounds of the train. He then playfully accuses Niko, stating, 'Niko's just taking a 5 pound off you.' Sharky, seated next to AJ, reacts with amusement, possibly exclaiming, 'You're joking.' AJ presses Niko, asking, 'Did you just give Niko some of your money?' to which Niko, holding the money, responds, 'Yeah. Because he asked for it,' eliciting laughter from the group, including Shroom who smiles. Meanwhile, Chunkz, wearing a white cap, excitedly reacts with an 'OH' and waves at a girl in a red 'HANNOVER' jersey walking down the aisle. The mock confrontation culminates with AJ declaring, 'Well done! Everyone round of applause for Niko for stealing money off kids!' and 'Absolutely incredible Niko, stealing money off kids,' as humorous on-screen text like 'AMAZING WORK' and 'ABSOLUTE LEGEND' appears. Sharky adds a sarcastic 'Disgusting behavior' before AJ asks, 'Alright, is the camera off?' The group's dynamic is characterized by exaggerated gestures and expressions, all within the train's blue and grey interior.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens inside a modern train, where Chunkz, in a black hooded jacket and white cap, animatedly holds a phone, engaged in a video call with a shirtless man who appears amused. They approach a man in a blue jacket and another in a white puffer jacket, playfully convincing them that a random bench is 'David Beckham’s Bench,' prompting laughter and a photo. The group then celebrates their successful prank with high-fives and smiles. The scene transitions to the interior of a black SUV, where Chunkz and AJ are seated in the back, laughing and discussing their next move. On-screen text reveals they are using a 'Mode of Transport: Aitch Car' as part of a challenge, with a progress indicator showing 3/5. They joke about the other team struggling and mention 'Niko got that sh*t granny walk as well,' referencing a previous interaction. The atmosphere is lighthearted and energetic, with the group clearly enjoying their urban adventure.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with Chunkz, AJ, Sharky, and Shroom walking briskly down a city street, dressed warmly in winter jackets and beanies. They are engaged in animated conversation, with one of them exclaiming, "Yeah, you can't even bot with them," as they discuss their mission to "complete challenges and get all the way to Timbuktu." Another adds, "I'm comfortable. You see the words I'm in right now? Crazy, yeah." A deep-voiced narrator then explains that "they are adamant on finding another source of transportation" and have asked the public where to find electric scooters. The group reacts with surprise and laughter, with one exclaiming, "Oh, maybe he was talking about that!" and another shouting, "Oh yeah, Doctor Scooter!" as they stop outside a shop window displaying electric scooters. On-screen text like 'THEY AREN'T REAL' and 'WAIT SO THE GUY WASN'T LYING' reflects their skepticism and curiosity, confirmed by their dialogue: "Wait, so the guy wasn't lying? He directed us to a scooter shop, thankfully." They decide to enter the brightly lit "Doctor Scooter" shop, filled with various models and accessories, where one of them suggests, "I'm saying, we just ask, can we have a trial run on the scooters? Just to see, we're interested in buying them. We're businessmen." Inside, they examine the modern-looking scooters closely, with one commenting on "the thickness in the tires." Another asks the Scooter Manager, "Can we just even round here, can we just try? Try one of your scooters?" as they prepare to ride one, while a group member mentions, "He's our boss, you know, Titan." The narrator concludes, "As the boys convince the manager to joyride one of the scooters..."

segments.integrated_caption: The minute begins inside a moving train, where Chunkz, AJ, Sharky, and Shroom are seated, engaging in light-hearted conversation. Chunkz, wearing a black hooded jacket with a 'drama' logo, initiates a playful challenge in his energetic voice, asking, "You know when Zone where Zone 1 ends, my friend?", to which AJ, in a black Jumpman cap, responds with a smiling "India." Sharky, in a black puffer jacket with a fur-lined hood, adds "Deansgate" as the group laughs. The train’s interior features blue patterned seats and yellow handrails, with a digital display showing 'St. Peter’s Square.' Upbeat, driving music begins around 0:13 as the scene transitions to an overcast outdoor setting in Manchester, where the group stands beside a black SUV. Chunkz, now in a black hoodie with the 'drama' logo, leans on the vehicle, announcing in his lively voice, "Alright, yo, there's only one person, the boys had to shout when he was in Manchester. Come on, come on." He reveals they're about to attempt a new challenge: he will ride a motorbike, exclaiming "Yes!" AJ, in a black and yellow patterned sweater and a New York Yankees cap, reacts with wide-eyed excitement, exclaiming "Tranquilo!" and gesturing animatedly, also adding, "Chunkz is going on the motorbike today." The group then moves into the SUV, where Chunkz and AJ are seen in close-up, laughing and joking. Chunkz, now in a white cap, playfully blames the driver for their predicament, stating in his voice, "We're currently in the middle of nowhere," with "no service" and "no signal." AJ, still in his Yankees cap, grins and echoes, "I'm blaming this guy right here," pointing at the driver, while also joking about "the traffic in Manchester." The mood remains playful and energetic, with on-screen text like 'I GENUINELY HEARD INDIA' and 'NO SERVICE' highlighting their comedic banter, as the lighting shifts from the bright train to the dim outdoor and dark car interior.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens inside a car with a shaky, close-up shot, where Chunkz, in a white cap, animatedly speaks, blaming others for challenges, while AJ, in a black Jumpman cap, reacts with wide-eyed laughter. Chunkz's voice is heard declaring, 'F*** bait squad,' followed by more laughter and him stating, 'It's all their fault.' A professional narrator's voice then introduces a satellite map of Manchester, showing a green route from Deansgate to Manchester Piccadilly, with Chunkz and AJ’s faces superimposed as avatars, explaining that 'this team is stuck in traffic somewhere, the other team has just arrived at Deansgate and find out that they actually haven't traveled far.' The scene transitions to Deansgate tram station, where the group disembarks from a tram, visibly surprised by the lack of people. The driver, with an amused voice, initially responds, 'You're joking,' but then, after Chunkz insists, 'I'm being serious,' the driver suggests, 'Let's have a nice road trip together.' AJ interjects with 'Somalian' before the scene ends with the group laughing and walking away, a 'HOME' sign visible in the background, as Chunkz exclaims, 'Woah!'

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with Chunkz, AJ, and Sharky inside a white car, marked with a yellow 'JET CARS' sticker, reacting with laughter and excitement. The Driver, a man in a blue jacket, smiles broadly as he announces their surprising destination: Scotland, prompting exclamations of disbelief and joy from the group. On-screen text highlights their reactions: 'THAT'S 6 HOURS AND A HALF', 'THE BIGGEST SEND', and 'OH MY GOD'. The Driver's voice is heard joking, "We are making a YouTube," and later, "I am not serious, man, get out, man, guys," followed by "Bye bye" and "Ciao guys" amidst the group's laughter. Chunkz exclaims, "The biggest send," while AJ asks, "You know how much he just sent us? Sent us!" After the car ride, the group exits onto an overcast city street in Manchester, walking along the sidewalk past brick buildings and a railway bridge. Chunkz, gesturing emphatically, recounts the experience, reflecting on the initial doubt, "You know since he said he can't take us, it felt like an energy just shifted in me. Four hours he's about to drive. There's no way we're gonna find someone to take us to Scotland." He adds, "I thought we had the biggest miracle of all time," to which AJ agrees, "Yeah, same." Chunkz continues, "I was on my knees, I said thank you for for... We we we hugged Sharky saying thank you. We're absolute idiots. We're idiots." segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a green location pin graphic confirming their arrival in Moston, Manchester, England, as a voice asks, "Where are we, bro?" and another (Chunkz) replies, "We're in Moston, right now." Time to jump in the car and get out of here!" He exclaims, "Oh my! Hey, look at this!" as he attempts to start the vehicle. The engine sputters and stalls, eliciting laughter and comments like "He stalled it!" and "No, no, no, he's coaching." The workshop, filled with tools and a 'FOR SALE' sign, buzzes with high energy as AJ continues to struggle with the vehicle, revving the engine which repeatedly stalls, met with shouts of "Hey!" and "Come on!" and playful accusations of "He stalled it, man!" and "Stalled it, too!" segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with AJ, wearing orange-tinted ski goggles and a black and yellow patterned sweater, in a dimly lit workshop, struggling to control a white and black Can-Am Maverick X3. The vehicle's engine revs loudly and struggles, making a 'vroom' sound, prompting AJ to exclaim 'AH' and 'SO HE'S' as he fumbles with the dashboard. A voice off-screen comments, 'That's not good.' AJ, visibly frustrated, shouts 'Ah, f***!' and then explains, 'Ah, no, so he's he's popped the drive shaft.' Chunkz, in a black hoodie with a 'drama' logo and a black New York Yankees cap, reacts with amusement, holding a phone and asking, 'Is that what it's called?' as the vehicle's engine continues to make struggling sounds. AJ, now in a black puffer jacket and white cap, exits the buggy, visibly flustered, shouting, 'What the hell happened?' and 'Why couldn't I drive it?' The group, including Chunkz and a man in a black beanie with a headlamp, gathers around, laughing, as someone declares, 'False alarm,' which another voice confirms, 'False alarm, fam.' AJ explains the issue, stating, 'He's done a he's done a... You know why I couldn't drive it? He put a two-wheel mode on.' He recounts hearing a distinct 'clunk' sound, which led him to believe, 'I said, yeah, something's broken.' A red and white graphic overlay, 'MODE OF TRANSPORT BUGGIES 4/5', appears. AJ, still in his white cap, declares, 'Alright, so that's the what's this called, a buggy?' with someone confirming, 'Buggy, yeah, yeah.' He then announces, 'Yeah, that was that's the buggy complete,' and outlines their next steps: 'We're gonna get some scran... and then we're gonna go uh maybe see a dog on the motorway.' The group laughs and moves on, with Chunkz exclaiming, 'That's Scottish!' and then urging, 'So let's go get some scran, man, let's go, let's get out of here,' followed by laughter. He also humorously adds, 'I thought it's mad 'cause I've killed chunks basically.' The scene transitions to a brightly lit street at night, outside 'THE DEANSGATE' pub, where Chunkz, holding a phone, announces, 'Okay, we actually have a development.'

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens with a nighttime street scene outside 'THE DEANSGATE' pub, where three men are engaged in an urgent conversation. The man on the left, wearing a black bucket hat with a Jordan logo and a 'Carbo' jacket, holds a phone and animatedly explains a 'development': they can be driven 100 miles, but need to cross the remaining 111 miles to 'Gretna Green' themselves, which he clarifies is 'on the edge of Scotland.' The man in the center, wearing a black beanie with a fur-lined hood, listens intently and asks, 'So we can cross the border ourselves?' The man on the right, in a black beanie and puffer jacket, holding a water bottle, reacts with surprise, asking, 'Never heard of Gretna Green. What's that?' He later urges, 'Call him, call him, let's let's let's negotiate. Get that hot chocolate.' The bucket-hatted man confirms they should call 'Mr. Broderick' who can help them, mentioning an offer of 'big money' and 'a car.' A narrator's voice-over explains, 'As the boys begin their negotiations with Mr. Broderick, they're quick to come to an agreement,' with garbled phone call audio confirming details like '45 minutes' and '100 miles.' The bucket-hatted man concludes, 'Well, I guess it's time for us to make our way to Broderick's.' The scene then transitions to an aerial view of Manchester, with a red dashed line indicating a route to Moston and text overlays emphasizing 'MEANING THAT TIME IS RUNNING OUT.' The narrator adds, 'Meanwhile, on the other side of Manchester, the boys arrive at another random location.' The video cuts to a dark, nighttime setting where a man in a black cap with an 'NY' logo and a black and yellow patterned sweater reacts with surprise, exclaiming, 'WHAT ARE YOU FOCUSING ON?' His surprise intensifies as the camera shows a small, brindle-colored French bulldog lying on the pavement, appearing to be in distress or playfully rolling, prompting the man to react with a scared 'Oh my god, oh my god, I'm scared. Ai yo yo yo,' creating a tense and urgent atmosphere with a mix of urban nightlife and a sudden, unexpected encounter.

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens with a man, later identified as Frank, in a black hoodie and bucket hat, excitedly pointing and announcing, "Here we are. Broderick's Love Coffee," as the on-screen text 'BRODERICKS LOVE COFFEE' appears. A collective "Ooh!" is heard as the camera cuts to the illuminated 'Broderick's Love Coffee' sign on a corrugated metal facade. A narrator then explains that "As the team arrives at Broderick's, they are taken on a tour of the facility and are given their long-awaited hot chocolate." Inside the cozy, modern café, Frank counts down, "Three, two, one, go!", before he and two other men, all in black puffer jackets and headwear, taste their hot chocolate from branded cups. They react with delighted "Ooh!" sounds, with one man in a fur-lined hood exclaiming, "That's good," and another with a water bottle adding, "That is good." Frank then declares, "That is the best hot chocolate I've ever had," prompting the man in the fur-lined hood to call him "a hot chocolate connoisseur." Frank agrees, "Oh yeah, yeah, to be fair, you would know." When asked by the man in the fur-lined hood to rank it, Frank rates it "a 9.8 out of 10," to which the man with the water bottle responds with an enthusiastic, "Solid. Wow. Absolutely incredible," and the man in the fur-lined hood adds, "That is incredible. Perfect temperature as well, you know." The conversation shifts as Frank asks, "So you said 100 miles you'd take us," clarifying he "drove 120 miles" for a similar experience. He then challenges them, "would you be willing to take us to Gretna Green which is 111 miles away?" The man in the fur-lined hood confidently replies, "We can do that 111 miles," with the man with the water bottle adding, "Not a problem. Absolutely amazing." The man in the fur-lined hood begins to discuss a "cut-off time of... which is 8 o'clock," before Frank interjects, "What's the time now?" The scene briefly cuts to a wider view of the café interior, showing a woman at the counter, before returning to the group’s animated discussion, with Frank holding a phone.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a navigation screen displaying a route to Preston, accompanied by a driving beat and a voice stating, "First time for everything," and "that's all our transportation complete." As the scene shifts to a nighttime urban setting, Chunkz, identifiable by his black cap with a white 'NY' logo and black-and-yellow patterned sweater, speaks to the camera, declaring, "Time to get out of Manchester. So let's hitchhike." He is joined by AJ, in a black puffer jacket and white cap, holding a phone. Chunkz repeatedly calls out "Gonna give us a ride?" to passing cars on the dimly lit street, illuminated by streetlights and headlights, with a blue 'Moston, Prestwich, and Local Shops' sign visible. As a car approaches, Chunkz, with increasing urgency, shouts, "Please! Slow down! Please!" A narrator comments on their rejections, and Chunkz laughs. The narrator then reveals, "Chunkz used his Insta live to his advantage," as Chunkz checks his phone, showing a live Instagram Reel with 283 likes, and pleads, "Anyone in Manchester that can help us out? We need help, please!" The group's desperation builds as they continue to wave down cars. Suddenly, a red car pulls over, and the Driver, wearing a gray hoodie, leans out, explaining, "I've just been watching the game at my house and I've just seen you on live Insta." Chunkz and AJ erupt in excited shouts and laughter, with Chunkz exclaiming, "Let's jump in, let's jump in!" and "Someone literally just pulled up!" They quickly get into the car, Chunkz calling the Driver "a hero." Inside, the Driver laughs and gestures as Chunkz, looking relieved, asks, "How far north can you take us?" as their journey continues.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a nighttime drive on a highway, where a blue sign for 'Gretna services' is visible. Inside the car, a man in a black puffer jacket with a fur-lined hood and black beanie (Man in Black Beanie with Fur-Lined Hood) drives, while a man in a black hoodie with a 'drama' logo (Chunkz) is seated beside him. The group initially jokes about "running south" before the driver suggests taking "the next exit." A man in a black bucket hat with a Jordan logo (Man in Black Bucket Hat with Jordan Logo) emphasizes their goal to be "at the most northern point of this station." As they exit at Gretna Green, Scotland, indicated by a green location pin graphic, excited voices, including Chunkz and a man in a black cap with an 'NY' logo (Man in Black Cap with NY Logo), repeatedly ask "Where's north?" The man in the black bucket hat directs them to "run this way," thanking "Danny." Outside, the group, including the Man in Black Cap with NY Logo and a man in a white 'NOCTA' cap (Man in White Cap, identified as André Onana), exits a white van. With a digital timer counting down from 3 minutes, the man in the black bucket hat urges them to "Run!" and the man in the black cap with NY logo notes they have "three minutes." Chunkz initially expresses reluctance, saying "I'm not going anywhere," prompting André Onana to ask "Why?" as Chunkz declares, "This is as far as I go." Despite this, the group runs through a dark, wooded area, crossing a narrow wooden bridge, with sounds of running and excited shouts of "Through the woods!" Chunkz exclaims "Uh-oh!" and "Oh my god!" while the man in the black cap with NY logo mentions "no booby traps." The man in the black bucket hat and Chunkz react with "Whoa! Holy! Oh my god!" to the terrain, with Chunkz finding "the hill's too big," which the man in the black cap with NY logo refutes. The man in the black bucket hat urges them to "Keep going!" as the timer shows "7:59." Heavy breathing and footsteps accompany their determined run. The man in the black cap with NY logo reiterates their mission, "What we're doing to go north, man." As the timer hits zero, the man in the black bucket hat exclaims "Oh, god!" and triumphantly announces, "Guys, we got to some random field in Gretna Green." They gather, laughing and celebrating, with one man holding a phone showing a map. André Onana, surprised, states, "In the middle of nowhere. Surely this has got to be enough," a sentiment echoed by the man in the black cap with NY logo. The man in the black bucket hat notes the time, "It's 8 PM," and André Onana exclaims "Oh my god!" upon seeing their location, marking their arrival in Gretna Green, Scotland, at the northernmost point of their journey.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens dramatically in a theatrical courtroom, featuring a judge at a wooden bench, a man in a black shirt gesturing from a witness stand, and two men in the audience observing intently. This tense scene is abruptly punctuated by a dramatic shout of "No!" followed by a sharp, impactful thud. A low, suspenseful drone then begins, building an atmosphere of anticipation as the scene quickly transitions to a vibrant, golden-lit vault. Inside this opulent setting, filled with stacks of gold bars and raining money, a man in a blue hoodie and black beanie stands holding a large pile of cash. He is soon joined by three other men: one in a pink shirt, another in a blue hoodie with a Nike logo, and a third in a black cap with an 'NY' logo. All four men smile confidently at the camera as prominent golden text appears on screen, declaring 'BETASQUAD COMPETE FOR £10,000'. The atmosphere shifts from the initial dramatic courtroom tension to one of opulent celebration and team unity, all while the underlying suspenseful drone continues to build, underscoring the high stakes of the competition.
segments.visual_original_caption: The video opens with a theatrical courtroom scene set against a dark backdrop, featuring a judge seated at a wooden bench, a man in a black shirt gesturing dramatically from a witness stand, and two other men seated in the audience—one in a blue hoodie and another in a black cap—observing intently. The scene quickly transitions to a vibrant, golden-lit vault filled with stacks of gold bars, where a man in a blue hoodie and black beanie stands holding a large pile of cash, with money raining down around him. He is soon joined by three other men: one in a pink shirt, another in a blue hoodie with a Nike logo, and a third in a black cap with an 'NY' logo. All four men smile confidently at the camera as golden text appears on screen: 'BETASQUAD COMPETE FOR £10,000', emphasizing the competitive and high-stakes nature of the event. The atmosphere shifts from dramatic and formal to celebratory and opulent, highlighting the prize and the team’s unity.
